commands.repair_reason: The remaining blockers are contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer handling of an external Vercel authorization status, not an automation-repairable code defect.; Cleared: The diff narrows a moderation/ownership bypass and does not add dependencies, CI changes, secret handling, publishing changes, or broader code execution surface. (sha=c7bd6726d76635217cc0aa376b638756eccd2426)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds moderation-state guards to direct and accepted skill ownership transfers, with regression tests, a changelog entry, and an ownership-transfer spec note.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows the direct transfer path reaches ownership writes with no moderation guard, while accepted transfers only cancel malicious/hidden/removed skills and miss active suspicious signals.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blockers are contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer handling of an external Vercel authorization status, not an automation-repairable code defect.

target.title: fix: block direct skill transfers under moderation
target.body:
## Summary

Blocks skill ownership transfers while the skill is under moderation or otherwise unavailable.

## What changed

- Direct owner moves are rejected when a skill is hidden, removed, suspicious, or malicious.
- Accepted pending-transfer flows run the same moderation guard before writing the new owner.
- Permission checks still happen before moderation-state checks so unauthorized users do not learn hidden/moderation state.

## Behavior proof

The focused transfer regression suite proves both transfer paths:

- Direct skill transfer rejects moderated skills.
- Accepted transfer rejects/cancels when the skill becomes moderated before acceptance.
- Normal authorization behavior remains intact for transfer cases outside moderation.

The explicit public behavior is failure under moderation, not silent ownership movement.

## Validation

```text
$ bun run test convex/skillTransfers.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       9 passed (9)
```

```text
$ bun run ci:static
passed
```

```text
$ bun run ci:unit
Test Files  204 passed (204)
Tests       1988 passed (1988)
Statements  85.98%
Branches    75.17%
Functions   86.37%
Lines        89.48%
```

```text
$ bun run ci:types-build
tsc, package typechecks, clawhub-mod typecheck, Vite build, and Nitro build passed.
```

## External blocker

The known Vercel preview failure is team authorization required for deployment. That is maintainer-owned and not a patch failure.

AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the diff, can maintain it, and validated it with the commands above.

commands.author: vyctorbrzezowski
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Claude live-session control_request/can_use_tool handling, maps allow/deny decisions from the resolved exec policy, and adds spawn-path regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows Claude live control_request frames hit the non-result early return without any control_response path, matching the linked stall report.
commands.review_followup: A focused repair can preserve explicit permission modes, narrow MCP auto-allow scope, and update regression tests on the PR branch.
